customer_segments: Art students aged 18-28 in MENA universities
Adult hobbyist painters aged 25-45 in Egypt, KSA, UAE
value_propositions: Curated, verified-quality art supplies delivered locally within MENA
Arabic-first UX with local payment methods (Fawry, Mada, KNET)
Creator community layer — follow artists, share work, get peer critiques
channels: WhatsApp Business for order confirmation and support
customer_relationships: Self-service marketplace with curated seller discovery
WhatsApp group communities per art medium (watercolour, oil, etc.)
Monthly email digest of new arrivals and featured artists

revenue_streams.name: Featured listing fee
revenue_streams.description: Sellers pay to appear first in search results for 7 days

revenue_streams.name: Premium seller subscription
revenue_streams.description: Verified seller badge + analytics dashboard + priority support

key_activities: Content creation (product showcases, tutorials, community posts)
key_partnerships: Local art supply importers and manufacturers

pricing_strategy.rationale: Price the commission slightly below Etsy (6.5%) but above free classifieds — positioned as 'premium curation with fair take rate'
pricing_strategy.discounting_policy: No blanket discounts — offer 1-month free premium to first 10 sellers to drive early supply
pricing_strategy.price_sensitivity_note: Student buyers are price-sensitive on products; sellers are price-sensitive on fees. Keep commission transparent and justify it with visibility and verified badge.

key_metrics: GMV (Gross Merchandise Value) — primary health indicator
Take rate (actual revenue / GMV) — measures monetisation efficiency
Monthly Active Sellers — supply side health
Monthly Active Buyers — demand side health
Repeat purchase rate (30-day) — measures community stickiness
CAC (Customer Acquisition Cost) — via Instagram spend / new buyers
Seller churn rate — measures supply satisfaction

business_model_risks.risk: Low take-rate tolerance — sellers may resist 12% and list elsewhere
business_model_risks.mitigation: Start at 8%, increase to 12% only after proving marketplace value (traffic, verified badge, analytics)

business_model_risks.risk: Chicken-and-egg problem — buyers leave if supply is thin, sellers leave if buyers are few
business_model_risks.mitigation: Launch supply-first: onboard 10 sellers before opening to buyers. Guarantee minimum visibility.

business_model_risks.risk: Payment gateway fees erode margin in low-AOV transactions
business_model_risks.mitigation: Set a $15 minimum order value. Offer cash-on-delivery as fallback.

founder_fit_assessment.reasoning: Marketplace model requires no software building — Sharetribe handles transactions. Founder's strengths in operations, community, and co-founder split cover all key activities.
founder_fit_assessment.biggest_execution_risk: Seller onboarding quality control — requires discipline and time in months 1-3

summary: A commission-based B2C marketplace is the right model for this stage — low upfront cost, scales with GMV, and doesn't require inventory. The 12% take rate is competitive and justified by curation value. The biggest model risk is the chicken-and-egg problem, which is mitigated by supply-first launch and guaranteed early seller visibility. Total fixed costs are under $130/month, making break-even achievable at modest GMV.
